characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears glasses and has beads strung in his mane. Big Eater: Loves eating meat more than anyone in his family. Good Parents: A great father to the cubs. Happily Married: To Cleo. Any scene Theo and Cleo share together is so wholesome. Even after having kids, their love is still so strong, they can't keep their paws off each other.

characters.name: Theo
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears glasses and has beads strung in his mane. Big Eater: Loves eating meat more than anyone in his family. Good Parents: A great father to the cubs. Happily Married: To Cleo. Any scene Theo and Cleo share together is so wholesome. Even after having kids, their love is still so strong, they can't keep their paws off each other.

characters.name: Cleo
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears earrings and a stack of necklaces. Good Parents: A great mother to the cubs. Shown Their Work: Most of Cleo's jobs, such as hunting, pouncing, and caring for the cubs are what lionesses do in real life.

characters.name: Lionel
characters.description: Big Brother Instinct: He does whatever he can to help Leona when she's in trouble. Half-Dressed Cartoon Animal: He wears a gray hat and a green-and-white-striped polo shirt. Heterosexual Life-Partners: His best friends are Lenny the Lizard and Gus the Rabbit. Straw Fan: Lionel loves the Cliff Hanger book series and is completely distraught when it almost comes to an end. He even goes so far as to call Cliff his idol.

characters.name: Leona
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears a necklace. Annoying Younger Sibling: In Lionel's eyes at least, since she pesters him to be her designated reader, but they still love each other. Cheerful Child: A hyperactive and playful lion cub.

characters.name: Click
characters.description: Animate Inanimate Object: A living computer mouse with the head of a rodent. She can drag and drop objects and characters into and out of books and websites. Nice Mice: Your friendly neighborhood computer mouse.

characters.name: Barnaby B. Busterfield III
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: Even when Walter and Clay aren't present, he will be making dry comments on the events of the episode. Do Not Call Me "Paul": He does not like to be called "Buster", which is what Walter and Clay call him. Hidden Depths:Despite being a sourpuss, Busterfield loves Fuzzy Wuzzy nursery rhymes and has a teddy bear he uses as aCompanion Cube.Sometimes, he is shown to actually value Walter and Clay as his friends. He likes to teach them different things. Despite being a sourpuss, Busterfield loves Fuzzy Wuzzy nursery rhymes and has a teddy bear he uses as aCompanion Cube. Sometimes, he is shown to actually value Walter and Clay as his friends. He likes to teach them different things.

characters.name: Walter and Clay Pigeon
characters.description: Finishing Each Other's Sentences: Neither of them can manage to say a complete sentence without the other's help. Only Friend: To Busterfield. Put on a Bus: They vanish after Season Four without a trace. Undying Loyalty: Despite Busterfield always getting annoyed with them, they take it in stride and do think of him as their friend. Vague Age: 'Farmer Ken's Puzzle' implies that the pigeons are roughly around Lionel's age, but their voices don't exactly match the standard seven-year-old child voice.

characters.name: Arty Smartypants
characters.description: Ambiguously Absent Parent: His father is never seen or mentioned. Breakout Character: Not only did he get his own music video, he stayed with the series until the end, unlike most of the other puppet characters. He even starred in a Ready to Learn short with the Cookie Monster. It's hard not to mention the show without him. Cloudcuckoolander: His whole personality revolves around him performing magic tricks with his magic smarty pants, dancing and word labels. Non-Standard Character Design: Whatever he is, he's certainly not your typical preschool show puppet.

characters.name: Marmy Smartypants
characters.description: Cool Old Lady: Marmy is just as eccentric as her sonny boy and hosts her own segment in later episodes.

characters.name: Heath the Thesaurus
characters.description: Advertised Extra: Despite appearing in the intro and being labeled as a main character on the defunct website, he only appears in two songs. He also introduced thelimericksin Season 1, and was in a few episodes in Seasons 2 and 3, but that was it. Drop-In Character: Whenever a character wants to know the meaning of a word, Heath will randomly pop up to explain. Vocal Evolution: When Heath was voiced by Tyler Bunch, he had a deep, manly voice. When Peter Linz took over, his voice became nasally and high-pitched.

characters.name: Information Hen
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears a shawl, a pair of glasses, and a headset. Cute Bookworm: A cute chicken who answers the phone and has information about the library, hens, or any other topic that callers have on their minds.

characters.name: Gus the Rabbit
characters.description: Bunnies for Cuteness: He doesn't look too bad, we'll give you that.

characters.name: Dr. Nitwhite and Watson
characters.description: Absent-Minded Professor: Nitwhite never gets his word discoveries right. Accidental Misnaming: Nitwhite is always accidentally referred to as "Dr. Nitwit" by other people. Berserk Button: Nitwhite does not take kindly to being referred to as "Dr. Nitwit". Unwitting Instigator of Doom: Kind of; Watson is usually the one who indirectly debunks Nitwhite's discoveries.

characters.name: The Vowelles
characters.description: Animate Body Parts: The Vowelles are different colored lips singing along with wigs. Animate Inanimate Object: The gloves and scarves are alive as well.

characters.name: Martha Reader
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Was one of the lead singers in the music video "We Are Family: A Musical Message for All." This was the only time she was seen outside her stage. No Celebrities Were Harmed: She's based on Martha Reeves, lead singer of theMotowngirl group Martha Reeves and the Vandellas.

characters.name: Johnny Consonatti
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Hardly appeared in season one, but then got promoted to the announcer for Martha's segments in the following season.

characters.name: Cliff Hanger
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: No matter what, he always returns to the cliff, humiliated and defeated. Catchphrase:Cliff:Can't... hold... on... much... LONGER! Dumb Muscle: Some episodes feature him getting rid of things that could help him get off the cliff, such as a phone or a parachute. Punny Name: He is in aLiteral Cliff Hanger. It's in his name and theme song. Victory Is Boring: The one time he got off the cliff and managed to stay off it, he found his retirement lacking in adventure to the point where he willingly returned to it.

characters.name: Chicken Jane
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Butt Chicken in this case. Always getting hurt at the expense of saving her owners Scot and Dot from their obliviousness. Undying Loyalty: Will always protect Scot and Dot, no matter what happens to her.

characters.name: Scot and Dot
characters.description: Children Are Innocent: They are completely oblivious to the obvious danger around them. The Ditzes: They are not very bright, which is why poor Chicken Jane has to save them in every episode. Too Dumb to Live: Even when they notice danger coming, they don't think to leave until Chicken Jane has to spell it out for them.

characters.name: Gawain and Knights
characters.description: Expy: The entire "Gawain's Word" segment is a big homage to the classic Saturday Night Live sketch, Wayne's World, right down to the surfer voice Gawain has, and the way the title card comes into frame. Surfer Dude: Gawain talks like this.

characters.name: The Un-People
characters.description: For the Evulz: Their whole reason for terrorizing poor little Monica.

characters.name: The Re-People
characters.description: Reset Button: These guys always save Monica from the Un-People to make everything go back to normal.

characters.name: Monica Maxwell
characters.description: Black and Nerdy: She's quite precocious for a little girl.

characters.name: Terry Bradshaw and Football Players
characters.description: Freeze-Frame Bonus: It's a hard-to-see detail even for the most eagle eyed viewer, but Terry Bradshaw's microphone has the series logo printed on the side of it. Iris Out: Each Blending Bowl segment ends this way before transitioning to the next segement. Large-Ham Announcer: As per the norm, Terry Bradshaw acts as one and does a fine job at it too.

characters.name: My Favorite Word opera singer
characters.description: Broken Record: Because the only words he sings about consist of fabulous, humungous, and miniscule, his singing about the definition of said words can be considered as such. Does This Remind You of Anything?: His outfit and hat in particular are aShout-Outto Napoleon Bonaparte.

characters.name: Silent E
characters.description: Anthropomorphic Typography: He's a sentient silent E represented as a man with a lower case e for a head. Cardboard Prison: The policeman is determined to lock him up, but he never stays incarcerated for long thanks to writing a letter asking that the cop give him an item he can use his silent e powers on to create a means of escaping his cell. Clothes Make the Superman: The "cap/cape" version of his segment has him turn the police officer's cap into a cape, which enables him to fly away after he puts it on. Conspicuous Trenchcoat: He wears an overcoat and a fedora to look all the more mysterious and sinister. Karma Houdini Warranty: After the usual "pin/pine" and "cap/cape" segments ended with him escaping his prison cell to cause more trouble, his final segment in "The Emperor's New Clothes/The Hungry Coat" ends with his body disappearing and becoming an ordinary lower case e after the police officer pursuing him captures him yet again, which can be interpreted as Silent E now being sent to solitary confinement to ensure he never again pulls any tricks to escape once more.
